metadata.title: Novel focal therapy treatment options for prostate cancer.
metadata.text: Examine and discuss novel focal therapy treatment options for prostate cancer.

metadata.title: Novel focal therapy treatment options for prostate cancer.
metadata.text: Focal therapy of prostate cancer is being actively investigated.

metadata.title: Photosensitizers in prostate cancer therapy.
metadata.text: The search for new therapeutics for the treatment of prostate cancer is ongoing with a focus on the balance between the harms and benefits of treatment.

metadata.title: The contemporary management of prostate cancer.
metadata.text: This review discusses the contemporary management of all stages of prostate cancer in the light of these modern developments, enabling holistic individualization of treatment, and describes the promise of future research to further improve outcomes.

metadata.title: Gene therapy for prostate cancer.
metadata.text: The greatest challenge in treating advanced prostate cancer is therapeutic access to and the elimination of metastases.

metadata.title: Prostate Cancer: Biology, Incidence, Detection Methods, Treatment Methods, and Vaccines.
metadata.text: Therapy for hormone- refractory prostate cancer is making progress, for almost all men with metastases will proceed to hormone-refractory prostate cancer.

metadata.title: The path forward in prostate cancer therapeutics.
metadata.text: The last decade has seen remarkable advances in the treatment of prostate cancer.

metadata.title: Combination Therapies in Locally Advanced and Metastatic Hormone-sensitive Prostate Cancer.
metadata.text: The treatment landscape for advanced prostate cancer has evolved significantly over the past decade.

metadata.title: Gene therapy for prostate cancer.
metadata.text: Prostate cancer gene therapy has recently transition from preclinical studies to clinical trials with the goal of developing novel treatments for prostate cancer.

metadata.title: Contemporary treatments in prostate cancer focal therapy.
metadata.study_type: Journal Article, Research Support, N.I.H., Extramural, Review
metadata.text: Prostate cancer focal therapy is an attractive option for well-selected patients because of its low complication profile; however, long-term oncologic outcome is still lacking and early recurrence rates are high, limiting the ability of most urologic associations from endorsing its routine use.

metadata.title: Gene therapy for prostate cancer.
metadata.text: This review details two aspects of prostate cancer gene therapy, the types of delivery systems under development and specific categories of therapeutic genes available with an emphasis on the mechanism of action of specific gene therapy strategies.

metadata.title: Focal therapy for prostate cancer.
metadata.text: Nowadays the treatment paradigm for localized prostate cancer is to distinguish patients with clinically relevant cancers who may benefit from radical treatment, or perhaps an organ-sparing approach, from the remainder who may not need intervention at the time of diagnosis.

metadata.title: Prostate cancer immunotherapy.
metadata.text: Medical treatment for prostate cancer (PC) targets hormonal pathways used by malignant cells.

metadata.title: Drug costs in the management of metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer in Canada.
metadata.study_type: Journal Article, Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't
metadata.text: The management of metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) is complex and the associated drug treatments are increasingly costly.

metadata.title: Prostate and metastasis-directed focal therapy in prostate cancer: hype or hope?
metadata.text: We searched the literature via PubMed, MEDLINE, and Embase for studies from 2014 to the present addressing focal therapy with non-palliative intent in metastatic prostate cancer patients, emphasizing prospective trials when available.

metadata.title: Combination Therapies in Locally Advanced and Metastatic Hormone-sensitive Prostate Cancer.
metadata.text: Articles were selected by the authors based on their scientific merit, clinical impact, and relevance to provide a summary of the evidence surrounding combination therapy in locally advanced prostate cancer and mHSPC.

metadata.title: Focal prostate cancer therapy in the era of multiparametric MRI: a review of options and outcomes.
metadata.text: The goal of prostate cancer focal therapy is to achieve oncologic control while reducing the rate of adverse events associated with whole-gland treatments.

metadata.title: Brachytherapy in Treating Patients With Prostate Cancer
metadata.text: PURPOSE: Phase II trial to study the effectiveness of brachytherapy in treating patients with stage I or stage II prostate cancer.

metadata.title: The contemporary management of prostate cancer.
metadata.text: The last three decades have seen rapid developments in the diagnostic and therapeutic landscape of prostate cancer, including multiparametric magnetic resonance imaging, positron emission tomography, robotic surgery, image-guided hypofractionated and stereotactic radiotherapy, novel anti-androgens and radioligand therapies.

metadata.title: Focal therapy for prostate cancer.
metadata.text: For a select cohort of patients with low-risk unifocal or unilateral prostate cancer lesions, a number of ablative treatment options for focal therapy are available with cryotherapy having the most clinical experience.

metadata.title: Radiation Therapy With or Without Androgen-Deprivation Therapy in Treating Patients With Prostate Cancer
metadata.text: PURPOSE: This randomized phase III trial is studying radiation therapy to see how well it works compared with radiation therapy given together with androgen-deprivation therapy in treating patients with prostate cancer.

metadata.title: Androgen deprivation and immunotherapy for the treatment of prostate cancer.
metadata.study_type: Journal Article, Review, Research Support, N.I.H., Extramural, Research Support, U.S. Gov't, Non-P.H.S.
metadata.text: The primary treatment for recurrent prostate cancer is androgen deprivation, and this therapy is typically continued lifelong for patients with metastatic prostate cancer.

metadata.title: Management of Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer (mHSPC): an Evolving Treatment Paradigm.
metadata.text: Combination systemic therapy is now standard of care for all men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer (mHSPC).

metadata.title: Prostate cancer.
metadata.study_type: Journal Article, Research Support, N.I.H., Extramural, Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't, Research Support, U.S. Gov't, Non-P.H.S., Review
metadata.text: Similarly, emerging research into novel therapeutics, such as targeted radioisotopes and immunotherapy, holds much promise for improving the lives of patients with prostate cancer.

metadata.title: MRI-guided focal therapy of prostate cancer.
metadata.text: Focal therapy affords a middleground option for patients with low- to intermediate-grade prostate cancer by providing a means of keeping their cancer at bay while avoiding the negative consequences of radical therapies.

metadata.title: Focal therapy for prostate cancer.
metadata.text: The concept of focal therapy is evolving with the understanding of the biologic variability (clinically aggressive, significant or insignificant) of various prostate cancer lesions that may require different treatment approaches.

metadata.title: Antiandrogen Therapy and Radiation Therapy With or Without Docetaxel in Treating Patients With Prostate Cancer That Has Been Removed by Surgery
metadata.text: This randomized phase II/III trial studies docetaxel, antiandrogen therapy, and radiation therapy to see how well it works compared with antiandrogen therapy and radiation therapy alone in treating patients with prostate cancer that has been removed by surgery.

metadata.title: Image-guided focal therapy for prostate cancer.
metadata.text: The purpose of this article is to review the existing literature regarding the basis of various focal therapy techniques such as cryotherapy, microwave, laser, and high intensity focused ultrasound, and to discuss the results of recent clinical trials that demonstrate early outcomes in patients with prostate cancer.

metadata.title: Photosensitizers in prostate cancer therapy.
metadata.text: This article will review newly developed photosensitizers under clinical trials for the treatment of prostate cancer, along with the potential advantages and disadvantages in delivering focal therapy.

metadata.title: Olaparib Monotherapy or in Combination with Abiraterone for the Treatment of Patients with Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer (mCRPC) and a BRCA Mutation.
metadata.text: Treatment strategies to improve outcomes in patients with metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) are evolving.

metadata.title: Novel focal therapy treatment options for prostate cancer.
metadata.text: With the widespread adoption of mpMRI-guided biopsies for prostate cancer, use of image-guided treatment of prostate cancer has increased.

metadata.title: Contemporary treatments in prostate cancer focal therapy.
metadata.study_type: Journal Article, Research Support, N.I.H., Extramural, Review
metadata.text: Despite the great variety of treatment techniques, each of these modalities is characterized by a significant rate of prostate cancer persistence within treatment zones (6-50%) and the presence of residual cancer within the prostate on rebiopsy (24-49%).

metadata.title: Treatment of Advanced Prostate Cancer.
metadata.text: The therapeutic landscape of prostate cancer has been transformed over the last decade by new therapeutics, advanced functional imaging, next-generation sequencing, and better use of existing therapies in early-stage disease.

metadata.title: Targeted therapy in the treatment of castration-resistant prostate cancer.
metadata.text: In the field of metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer, a bevy of novel therapeutics have recently been proven to extend survival via distinct mechanisms of action.

metadata.title: Standard Systemic Therapy With or Without Definitive Treatment in Treating Participants With Metastatic Prostate Cancer
metadata.text: This phase III trial studies how well standard systemic therapy with or without definitive treatment (prostate removal surgery or radiation therapy) works in treating participants with prostate cancer that has spread to other places in the body.

metadata.title: Contemporary treatments in prostate cancer focal therapy.
metadata.study_type: Journal Article, Research Support, N.I.H., Extramural, Review
metadata.text: High-intensity focal ultrasound, focal laser ablation, irreversible electroporation, focal cryotherapy, and photodynamic therapy have been used as treatment modalities for localized prostate cancer treatment.

metadata.title: Advances in Targeted Therapy for Metastatic Prostate Cancer.
metadata.text: Over the past few years, treatment for advanced prostate cancer has begun shifting away from a one-size-fits-all approach toward biomarker-based therapies for select groups of patients.

metadata.title: Prostate cancer immunotherapy.
metadata.text: Intense research performed on immunotherapy for prostate cancer.Vaccine therapy with sipuleucel-T, the only approved immunotherapy for prostate cancer.Ipilimumab shows survival benefits.Olaparib shows survival benefits.Findings should be confirmed on further trials to identify target population characteristics and proper disease stage.Immunotherapy is not yet a standard due to tumor environment complex interaction between immune system and malignant cells.

metadata.title: Prostate Cancer: Biology, Incidence, Detection Methods, Treatment Methods, and Vaccines.
metadata.text: A shortened course of hormonal therapy with docetaxel following radical prostatectomy (or radiation therapy) for high-risk prostate cancer has been shown to be both safe and feasible.

metadata.title: Management of Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer (mHSPC): an Evolving Treatment Paradigm.
metadata.text: Based on the Chemohormonal Therapy Versus Androgen Ablation Randomized Trial for Extensive Disease in Prostate Cancer (CHAARTED) study subgroup analysis, chemohormonal therapy may be most beneficial in men with high-volume disease burden, as men with low-volume metastatic disease do not appear to experience a survival benefit with chemohormonal therapy, while abiraterone in combination with ADT appears to be beneficial across both disease volume subgroups.
